tasks.title: Scaffold revise-pr SKILL.md with harvest stage
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skills/revise-pr/SKILL.md with frontmatter (name, description, slug, model, effort) and the full harvest stage. Step 1 — input: accept a PR URL argument; when no URL given, fallback to `gh pr list --author @me --state open` in cwd and prompt user to pick. Step 2 — repo assertion: resolve the PR's repo from `gh pr view <url> --json headRepository` and compare to the cwd repo from `git remote get-url origin`; bail immediately with a clear error message if they differ. Step 3 — GraphQL harvest: call `gh api graphql` to fetch all review threads via `reviewThreads(first:100)` (capturing `isResolved`, `comments.nodes[].{body,author.login,url,databaseId}`), all review summary bodies (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT), and top-level pull request comments. Apply harvest filter: keep only threads where `isResolved === false`, exclude comments where `author.login` matches the authenticated user (from `gh api user`), and include bugbot comments by known bot login as a first-class source regardless of other filters. Detect `Resource not accessible by integration` error on GraphQL response and print the exact recovery command `gh auth refresh -h <hostname>` before stopping. All behavior is via inline skill instructions; no separate helper scripts needed for this slice.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md exists at the correct path with valid frontmatter (name, description, slug, model, effort fields present)
Skill instructions describe the fallback `gh pr list --author @me --state open` flow when no PR URL argument is given
Skill instructions describe the repo assertion check and state that a mismatch prints a clear error and stops immediately
Skill instructions include the complete GraphQL query structure for reviewThreads, review bodies, and PR comments
Harvest filter logic is stated explicitly: isResolved===false, exclude self-authored, include bugbot by login
SSO 403 handling is described: detect 'Resource not accessible by integration' and print `gh auth refresh -h <hostname>` recovery command
A synthetic dry-run instruction set (run skill on a real or test PR) produces a harvested item list with correct structure

tasks.title: Add provisional triage stage to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with the provisional triage stage (pipeline step 2). After harvest output is displayed, the skill auto-classifies each item using a two-axis model. Axis 1 — class: one of `bug` / `change` / `question` / `diligence` / `discuss` / `nit`. Axis 2 — escape-disposition (bug class only): `confirmed_escape` / `false_flag` / `not_an_escape` / `unverified` (skeptical default; always start here). Present a triage table to the user with columns: item number, thread/comment excerpt, proposed class, proposed disposition (bug class only). Instruct the skill to accept user overrides for any row before proceeding to diligence. State that the table is a proposal only and that no action is taken until the HITL gate.
acceptance_criteria: Skill instructions describe the two-axis classification model with all valid class values and all valid disposition values
Triage table format is described with at minimum: item number, excerpt, proposed class, proposed disposition columns
Escape-disposition column appears only for bug-class items
Instructions state that `unverified` is the skeptical default for escape-disposition (not `confirmed_escape`)
Instructions state the triage table is a proposal and user may override any row before diligence runs
Instructions state that no action fires until the HITL gate in a later step

tasks.title: Add diligence gate to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with the mandatory universal diligence gate (pipeline step 3). The gate is non-optional: every harvested item must pass through it with no bypass path. For each item, surface three pieces of context side by side: (1) the flagged claim text verbatim, (2) the originating comment URL, (3) the relevant code context from the PR diff or referenced files. The user renders a verdict for each item in v1 (manual diligence). Valid verdicts: `confirmed_escape`, `false_flag`, `not_an_escape`, or `unverified`. Reconcile: diligence may promote an item (e.g. a `question` that turns out to be a real bug) or demote it (e.g. a `bug` that is a false flag). Instructions must state that no item may exit the gate without an explicit verdict — partial completion is not allowed. Note that the `vet` skill slots into this gate in a future version.
acceptance_criteria: Instructions state explicitly that the gate is mandatory and every item must pass through it — no bypass path
Instructions describe the three context surfaces per item: claim text, comment URL, relevant code context
All four valid diligence verdicts are listed: confirmed_escape, false_flag, not_an_escape, unverified
Instructions state that a promoted or demoted classification after diligence is valid and should update the triage table
Instructions state that partial completion (some items not reviewed) is not allowed before proceeding to the HITL gate
Instructions note that `vet` skill slots into the same gate when built

tasks.title: Add HITL confirmation gate + attribution-tracer invocation to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with pipeline steps 5 and 6. Step 5 — HITL confirmation gate: present the evidence-backed final categorization (post-diligence) as a confirmation table. User confirms or overrides each item's final class and disposition before any action proceeds. This is the last human checkpoint before actions fire. Step 6 — attribution-tracer invocation: after the HITL gate, for each item whose final disposition is `confirmed_escape`, spawn one `attribution-tracer` subagent with inputs: `issue_description` (the confirmed defect claim text), `pr_url` (the PR URL from Step 1), `branch` (the PR head branch from `gh pr view --json headRefName`), `transcript_path` (absolute path to current session transcript). Attribution fires once per `confirmed_escape` item, not once per session. Items with disposition `false_flag`, `not_an_escape`, or `unverified` must never trigger attribution-tracer. The `fix` field in attribution records will be empty/TBD since no fix exists yet; this is intentional. Attribution demotes to candidate/improves:null when the escape is ambiguous. Include the invariant explicitly: skill never makes outward HTTP writes (no GitHub comment posting, no thread resolution) during its own run.
acceptance_criteria: Instructions describe the HITL confirmation table and state it is the last checkpoint before any action fires
Instructions state attribution-tracer fires once per confirmed_escape item, not once per session
Instructions list the exact subagent inputs: issue_description, pr_url, branch, transcript_path
Instructions explicitly state that false_flag, not_an_escape, and unverified items never trigger attribution-tracer
Instructions note that the fix field will be empty/TBD and that this is intentional
Instructions state the skill never makes outward HTTP writes during its own run

tasks.title: Add seed stop + provenance block + relay behavior + learning-capture tail to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with pipeline step 7 and the closing blocks. Step 7 — seed stop: after attribution-tracer runs for all confirmed_escape items, invoke `/to-seed` passing the session context including a structured provenance block. Build the provenance block as: `{pr_url: string, head_branch: string, items: Array<{thread_node_id: string, final_class: string, disposition: string}>}`. Skill stops at the seed — it does not invoke /to-tasks, /run-tasks, branch checkout, or push. State this boundary explicitly. Relay stub behavior (inline v1): after /to-seed completes, read `seed.provenance.items[]`, filter to non-code classes (question, discuss, nit), and print a formatted list to the user. No external calls or GitHub posting from this step. Add the standard managed learning-capture tail block (matching the pattern used in other SKILL.md files) with skill slug `revise-pr` and supported triggers. The final closing section of the skill should list next-step suggestions: /to-tasks when the seed is ready for implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Provenance block shape is specified exactly: {pr_url, head_branch, items[{thread_node_id, final_class, disposition}]}
Instructions state explicitly that the skill stops after /to-seed and does not invoke /to-tasks, /run-tasks, branch checkout, or push
Relay stub section filters to question/discuss/nit classes only and states no external calls are made
Learning-capture tail block is present with correct skill slug 'revise-pr'
Next-step suggestion '/to-tasks' appears in the closing section
Full SKILL.md is reviewed end-to-end and all seven pipeline steps are present in correct order

tasks.title: Wire revise-pr into schema, pipeline, and skill registry
tasks.description: Three infrastructure changes plus skill registration. (1) Schema: add `revise-pr` to the `producer` enum in ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/contracts/provenance-schema.json. (2) Pipeline: add a `revise-pr` node to ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skill-pipeline.json with edges `{skill:'to-tasks', when:'seed is verified and ready for implementation'}` and `{skill:'relay', when:'non-code items need user notification'}`. Also add stub nodes for `vet` (next:[]), `relay` (next:[]), and `investigate` (next:[]) — these are wiring anchors; none are functionally implemented. (3) Stub skill directories: create minimal SKILL.md stub files at skills/vet/SKILL.md, skills/relay/SKILL.md, and skills/investigate/SKILL.md — each with a frontmatter header and a one-line description stating it is a stub. These are required because inject-learning-tail.py validates that every slug referenced in pipeline 'next' edges has a corresponding directory under claude-code-shared/skills/. (4) Run: `python ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/inject-learning-tail.py revise-pr` (or equivalent invocation) to bake the closing next-step suggestion into SKILL.md from the pipeline graph. (5) Run `/register-skill` to add revise-pr to the skill registry (settings.json). Validate each change: run `bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh --instance ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/contracts/provenance-schema.json /dev/null` to verify the schema still parses; confirm skill-pipeline.json is valid JSON with `python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))' skill-pipeline.json`.
acceptance_criteria: 'revise-pr' appears in the producer enum of provenance-schema.json
validate-schema.sh exits 0 after the schema change
skill-pipeline.json is valid JSON after additions (python3 json.load check passes)
revise-pr node exists in skill-pipeline.json with edges to to-tasks and relay
vet, relay, and investigate stub nodes exist in skill-pipeline.json with next:[]
Stub SKILL.md files exist for vet, relay, and investigate under claude-code-shared/skills/
inject-learning-tail.py completes without error on revise-pr SKILL.md
revise-pr appears in the skill registry after /register-skill runs
